name: LangChain Core
what_it_does: Core abstractions package for LangChain providing the Runnable interface, LCEL chain composition with the | operator, and base classes (BaseLLM, BaseRetriever, BaseTool) that all LangChain integrations implement.
use_cases: Compose multi-step agent pipelines using LCEL pipe syntax (prompt | llm | parser) with built-in streaming, async, and batch execution
Build portable agent components that work across any LLM provider by coding against BaseLLM/BaseChatModel rather than provider-specific SDKs
Use invoke/ainvoke/stream/astream/batch methods uniformly across all Runnables to simplify agent orchestration code
Implement RunnableParallel to fan out agent sub-tasks concurrently and merge results without manual asyncio boilerplate
Attach callbacks and tracers to any Runnable for agent observability — token counts, latency, and intermediate outputs — without modifying chain logic
not_for: Direct LLM provider API calls — langchain-core provides only the interface layer; install provider-specific packages (langchain-openai, langchain-anthropic) separately
Vector store operations or document loading — those live in langchain-community or provider packages, not langchain-core
Teams that want minimal dependencies — importing langchain-core pulls in its abstraction hierarchy even for simple use cases
best_when: You are building a multi-step agent that needs composable, testable pipeline components with uniform streaming/async/batch execution across swappable LLM providers.
avoid_when: You need a simple one-shot LLM call and want zero framework overhead — use the provider SDK directly; LangChain Core's abstractions add indirection that complicates debugging.

auth.notes: langchain-core itself has no auth; authentication is handled by each provider integration package via environment variables (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.).

pricing.paid_tiers: None for langchain-core; LangSmith tracing platform has paid tiers
pricing.notes: langchain-core is free and open source. LangSmith (optional observability platform) has a free tier and paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: None (library — limits come from underlying LLM providers)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors from provider integrations are re-raised through LangChain's abstraction layer; stack traces are verbose and can obscure the root cause; check the innermost exception.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LCEL chains are stateless by design; idempotency is determined by the underlying LLM provider.
known_agent_gotchas: langchain-core provides interfaces only — you must install separate packages (langchain-openai, langchain-anthropic, etc.) to get actual LLM implementations; missing packages give confusing ImportError messages
LCEL's | operator creates implicit chain composition that can be hard to debug; intermediate outputs are not logged unless a tracer/callback is explicitly attached
The Runnable.invoke() method is synchronous but many provider implementations use async internally; mixing sync and async code requires careful event loop management
RunnablePassthrough and RunnableLambda have subtle input/output type coercion behaviors that can cause silent data shape mismatches between chain steps
LangChain's rapid version cadence means patterns documented in tutorials may use deprecated APIs; always verify against the installed version's API reference

security_score_components.security_notes: No direct security surface in the core library. Provider API keys flow through environment variables or runnable config; ensure they are not serialized into chain config objects that might be logged or stored.
